output.code:
import re, json, sys, textwrap

with open('/mnt/data/file-2Tqm6owbUqZit3Ap3FV4qr-text_to_analyse.txt', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()

# Find double-quoted spans
dq_spans = [(m.start(1), m.end(1), m.group(1)) for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', text)]
dq_spans

import re

text = open('/mnt/data/file-2Tqm6owbUqZit3Ap3FV4qr-text_to_analyse.txt','r',encoding='utf-8').read()

citations = []

# A & B: quoted verses in the main body (balanced quotes)
for m in re.finditer(r'قال تعالى:\s*"([^"]+)"', text):
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    citations.append(('Aya_or_partial', start, end, m.group(1)))

# C: unbalanced quote before References. Find the last occurrence of 'حيث قال تعالى: "' after index ~1300, then end before '\nReferences:'
ref_idx = text.find('\nReferences:')
# Find the last opening quote before ref_idx after the 'بالإضافة' segment
unbalanced_start_pat = 'بالإضافة إلى ذلك، حرم الله تعالى نكاح النساء بالمصاهرة، حيث قال تعالى: "'
start_index = text.find(unbalanced_start_pat)
if start_index != -1:
    # opening quote at:
    open_quote_idx = start_index + len(unbalanced_start_pat) - 1  # this is position of starting quote char? Actually last char is the double-quote
    # Actually start of content is open_quote_idx+1
    content_start = open_quote_idx + 1
    # content end: if there is a closing quote before ref_idx, else up to ref_idx-1
    closing_quote_idx = text.find('"', content_start, ref_idx)
    if closing_quote_idx == -1:
        content_end = ref_idx  # exclude newline start
    else:
        content_end = closing_quote_idx
    citations.append(('Aya_or_partial_unbalanced', content_start, content_end, text[content_start:content_end]))

# D: hadith after 'حديث:' without quotes, in [1]
for m in re.finditer(r'حديث:\s*([^"\.\n]+)\.', text):
    # but this also catches the [2] one, which is not? there it's followed by quote not period; our regex will skip over quotes
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    citations.append(('Hadith_plain', start, end, m.group(1).strip()))

# E: ayah after 'قال تعالى:' without quotes, in [1] before '{'
for m in re.finditer(r'قال تعالى:\s*([^{\[\n]+?)\s*\{', text):
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    citations.append(('Aya_plain_before_brace', start, end, m.group(1).strip()))

# F: hadith in quotes in [2]
for m in re.finditer(r'حديث:\s*"([^"]+)"', text):
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    citations.append(('Hadith_quoted', start, end, m.group(1).strip()))

# G: ayah in parentheses in [2]
for m in re.finditer(r'قال تعالى:\s*\(([^)]+)\)', text):
    start = m.start(1)
    end = m.end(1)
    citations.append(('Aya_in_parens', start, end, m.group(1).strip()))

len(citations), citations[:10]

# Verify slices match
for typ, start, end, content in filtered:
    assert text[start:end] == content, (typ, start, end)
len(filtered)

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
